source_title: DEXOP: A Device for Robotic Transfer of Dexterous Human Manipulation

ai_summary.one_liner: DEXOP is a passive exoskeleton that mechanically links human and robot fingers, enabling direct-contact dexterous demonstration collection at up to 8× the speed of teleoperation.
ai_summary.problem: Dexterous robot manipulation requires high-quality demonstrations of complex multi-finger tasks, but teleoperation is slow (seconds per action), lacks natural contact feedback, and produces noisy data for contact-rich manipulation.
ai_summary.solution: A passive exoskeleton (DEXOP) with 4-bar linkages directly coupling human and robot finger joints, requiring no actuation or calibration. Humans manipulate objects naturally while the robot hand mirrors their movements; tactile sensors provide supplementary force data.
ai_summary.key_result: DEXOP achieves up to 8× higher task throughput than teleoperation (bulb install: 11s vs 86s); policies trained on 160 DEXOP + 40 TeleOP demos achieve 51.3% vs 42.5% success for pure TeleOP, collected in half the time.
limitations: Requires per-robot-hand co-design; current EyeSight hand lacks full human hand DOF for in-hand reorientation
Provides proprioceptive but not tactile feedback to the human operator

research_gaps.question: 정책 평가가 단일 태스크(전구 설치)에 집중되어 있어, 더 다양한 태스크에서 DEXOP 데이터의 품질 이점이 일반화되는지 확인이 필요하다
